---
title: Domestic migration patterns shift toward Rural areas
description: Relative change in domestic net migration for rural and nonrural counties,
  indexed to 2007, excluding international migration.
date: '2026-05-07'
layout: default
permalink: /charts/rural-economic-outlook-3-4-net-migration-since-gr-lc-domestic-only/
categories: []
tags: []
image: 3.4_net_migration_since_GR_lc_domestic_only.svg
chart:
  title: Domestic migration patterns shift toward Rural areas
  chartType: line
  interactive: false
  source: Internal Revenue Service
  sourceUrl: https://www.irs.gov/statistics/soi-tax-stats-migration-data
  featured: false
producedBy: rural-economic-outlook
usesDatasets:
  - irs-migration
usesPackages:
  - ruraldefinitions
usesResources: []
format:
  gfm:
    toc: false
    wrap: none
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

![Uses IRS SOI county-to-county migration data to track domestic net migration for rural and nonrural counties since 2007, isolating domestic flows from international migration to focus on internal U.S. redistribution.](./3.4_net_migration_since_GR_lc_domestic_only.svg)

## Overview

Uses IRS SOI county-to-county migration data to track domestic net migration for rural and nonrural counties since 2007, isolating domestic flows from international migration to focus on internal U.S. redistribution.

## Key Findings

- Rural domestic migration turned positive in the post-COVID period, reversing a decade of net outflow.
- Nonrural areas saw domestic migration moderate after the pandemic remote-work wave.
- The IRS SOI data captures migrating tax filers and their dependents — it proxies working-age household moves.
- International migration is excluded; adding it would shift levels but not the rural-nonrural divergence.

## Reproducibility

Generated by `R/viz/presentation/net_migration_lc_domestic_only.R` in the producing project.
